title: Military Programs
text:
Military Fellows Program
Lincoln Laboratory provides fellowships to active-duty U.S. military officers who are enrolled in a graduate school program, often at MIT, or are completing requirements for advanced education at Senior Service Schools.
For graduate students, the military fellowships cover tuition and fees and require that the students perform thesis research at Lincoln Laboratory. Officers enrolled in a Service School program do research at the Laboratory while taking courses in national security management at MIT.
Each summer, Lincoln Laboratory also employs undergraduate students from the United States service academies. Interns spend three to six weeks of their summer contributing to sponsored projects under the guidance of their assigned mentors.
If you are interested in becoming a Lincoln Laboratory Military Fellow, please fill out the application below.
The application window for the AY 2026–2027 Lincoln Laboratory Military Fellows Program is now open.
Application for Academic Year 2026–2027
For further information, contact [email protected] or 781-999-4060.
SkillBridge
Lincoln Laboratory is an authorized provider organization for SkillBridge, a Department of Defense program connecting active-duty service members with internships, apprenticeships, and job/employment skills training in civilian career areas. The program is designed to help service members transition to civilian employment once their military duty ends. Active-duty personnel can spend up to the last 180 days of their military service at the Laboratory, gaining real-world experience matched to their backgrounds and skillsets while continuing to receive military compensation and benefits. After completing the program, SkillBridge participants are encouraged to apply for employment at Lincoln Laboratory, which is committed to recruiting, employing, and retaining U.S. military veterans. To apply for a SkillBridge opportunity at the Laboratory, fill out the application below.
Apply Here
For further information, contact [email protected] or 781-999-4060.
